source.title: A prefrontal-thalamo-hippocampal circuit for goal-directed spatial navigation

confidence_rationale: Provides foundational circuit evidence that hippocampal CA1 requires prefrontal input (via nucleus reuniens) to represent goal-directed trajectories rather than just instantaneous position. Trajectory-dependent CA1 firing is directly relevant to MECH-230's claim that hippocampal terrain navigation can be oriented toward goal states. Confidence is somewhat reduced because the paper does not address the latent sub-space geometry of goal representations per se, nor approach/harm separation -- it establishes the circuit requirement without characterizing the representational structure of the goal state itself.
failure_signatures: If E3-to-hippocampus goal seeding in REE fails to produce trajectory-dependent navigation, the prefrontal driving input modeled in this paper is not being correctly instantiated.
If hippocampal terrain navigation in REE operates correctly without an active z_goal signal, the trajectory-dependence observed here would be dissociable from z_goal, weakening MECH-230.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Medial prefrontal cortex projects via nucleus reuniens to CA1, and this projection is necessary for trajectory-dependent firing in CA1 during goal-directed navigation. Lesion or optogenetic silencing of nucleus reuniens abolishes trajectory-dependent activity in CA1 but not CA3 (which lacks nucleus reuniens input). The prefrontal-hippocampal circuit encodes the path toward a goal, not just current position.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-230 requires that hippocampal terrain navigation is oriented toward z_goal states maintained by E3. Ito et al. establish the biological substrate: prefrontal cortex (E3 analogue) must actively transmit goal-path information to hippocampus (ARC-007 terrain navigator) via a thalamic relay for trajectory-dependent navigation to occur. Without E3's goal signal, hippocampus defaults to positional encoding without prospective goal orientation. This is the circuit-level substrate for z_goal enabling terrain navigation toward goal states.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper characterizes the *circuit requirement* for goal-directed trajectory firing, not the latent representational geometry of the goal state. It does not show that goal representation is a positive attractor distinct from harm -- only that prefrontal input is required for prospective goal-path encoding. The thalamic relay (nucleus reuniens) in biological circuits has no explicit counterpart in current REE architecture, though the functional requirement (E3 goal signal driving hippocampal prospection) is present.
mapping.source_context: Rat in vivo electrophysiology (single-unit recording); T-maze and W-maze spatial navigation with reward-directed trajectories; lesion and optogenetic silencing of nucleus reuniens.

confidence_components.notes: Highest source quality (Nature, Moser lab, in vivo electrophysiology with causal intervention). Mapping fidelity moderate: establishes the circuit requirement but not the sub-space geometry MECH-230 specifically tests. Transfer risk moderate-high: rat in vivo to REE's discrete latent architecture; thalamic relay has no explicit REE equivalent. This paper is best understood as foundational circuit evidence that E3 goal-signal is necessary for hippocampal terrain navigation, not as direct evidence for z_goal attractor structure.
